stories.title: Students put Senedd hopefuls on the spot in youth-led Cambria debates
stories.summary: Learners at Coleg Cambria didn’t just attend political hustings ahead of the Senedd Elections on May 7 – they ran them. In a pair of events held at the college’s Wrexham Yale and Deeside sites, students chaired debates, set the agenda and put candidates from political parties directly on the spot. Representatives from each party

stories.title: FOCUS Wales Film Festival launches feature film competition for Welsh filmmakers
stories.summary: FOCUS Wales Film Festival has announced the launch of its first feature film competition, marking an expansion of the BAFTA Cymru-qualifying event. The festival, taking place in Wrexham next month, said the new competition aims to support Welsh filmmaking and provide a platform for films to reach wider audiences. stories.title: PayPal users warned over 1p deposit scam that triggers fake phone calls
stories.summary: PayPal users are being warned to be on their guard after scammers found a way to trigger genuine emails from the platform as part of a phone fraud. Which? is alerting users to a scam in which fraudsters deposit 1p into a target’s PayPal account through a fake or compromised business profile. The deposit generates

stories.title: How Marketing Agencies Scale Influencer Campaigns Without Losing Quality
stories.summary: When an agency runs one influencer campaign, gut feel can carry you through. At ten campaigns, patterns emerge. At fifty, gut feel becomes a liability and the cracks in your process become revenue problems. Why scaling breaks most agency workflows Influencer marketing looks manageable until the active brief list hits double figures. At that point,

stories.title: Police renew appeal over Buckley electric motorbike robbery after two arrests
stories.summary: North Wales Police are renewing an appeal for information following a robbery in Buckley last month in which a victim was forced off an electric motorbike.
